Position 21 activation excursion — steps [21, 22, 23]

What am I looking at?
Canvas position 21 in the mxfp4 run is decoded below. The middle step is where the chosen token flips; the flanking steps are the stable before/after. Each chart pins this one position and walks it down the network, so where the red (flip) line departs from the green/blue lines is the layer that injected the divergence. Per-channel charts then show which channels carry the spike — the fp4 block-scale lever.

Decoded token at this position

steproletokenprobentropytop-5
21before' scat'1.0000.001' scat'=1.00 ' scattered'=0.00 ' scatter'=0.00 ' dispers'=0.00 ' scattering'=0.00
22FLIP' scat'1.0000.000' scat'=1.00 ' spraw'=0.00 ' dispers'=0.00 ' 가'=0.00 'ters'=0.00
23after' scat'1.0000.000' scat'=1.00 'ters'=0.00 ' scram'=0.00 ' sc'=0.00 ' opposite'=0.00

qkv_proj

L2 norm of this position's activation vector feeding the GEMM. The overall magnitude of the token. A step-32 spike here vs step 31/33 means the whole activation for this position blew up at this layer.
Largest single |value| in this position's activation vector. This is the number fp4 must represent for this token; a spike forces a coarse block scale and is the direct quantization-error source.
Peakedness of this position's activation (Gaussian = 3). High = the token's energy is concentrated in a few channels -- spiky vectors are exactly what fp4's 32-wide blocks quantize worst.

Channel context at the divergence layer

For each site, the layer where the flip-step tok_absmax spiked most is shown. The spectrum overlays per-channel absmax for the three steps; the table lists channels whose flip-step absmax most exceeds the neighbor steps. Compare each channel's absmax to the block median (blk_p50): a channel ≫ blk_p50 forces a coarse fp4 scale on its 32-channel block — the concrete place to spend precision.
